title: 破镜谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\137-破镜谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Mrs. Bantry recounts the details of the gathering at which Heather Badcock died. The attendees included Marina Gregg, her husband Jason Radcliffe, public relations secretary Eileen Jelinski, a vicar, Mrs. Sandlerford, Colonel and Mrs. Corrington, the county magistrate, and various representatives from a movie studio. During the event, Heather Badcock, the secretary of the St. John Ambulance, spoke bluntly to Marina Gregg about a meeting they had shared years prior. This interaction caused Marina to freeze with a shocked expression while staring fixedly at a reproduction of Bellini's Madonna painting on the wall. While Marina was distracted, several people, including the town mayor and his wife, a young man with a mustache, and a girl with a camera, ascended the stairs. Jason Radcliffe eventually nudged Marina back to her usual charming self and served champagne to Marina and Mrs. Baskerville. Shortly after, a guest felt unwell.

An autopsy revealed that Heather Badcock died from four grains of a benzodiazepine-related drug, though the method of administration was unknown. Following a two-week investigation, Inspector Frank Cornish visited Arthur Badcock. While driving Arthur home to Arlington Lane, Cornish observed Arthur's neighbor, Mrs. Mary Bain, who had moved there six months prior following her husband's death. Cornish noted Mrs. Bain's alert, cautious gaze and suspected she had a prior connection to the police. After she served them tea and departed, Cornish continued his questioning of the agitated Arthur Badcock regarding the autopsy results.
